---
species: "Colpomenia peregrina"
accepted_code: "COPE"
alternate_codes: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
diminutive: #"CODE" # uncomment and fill in if one exists
alternate_diminutives: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
common_name: "Sea potato or Oyster thief"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Brown algae that forms thin potato-like sacs" # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/colpomenia_peregrina_M.D. Guiry_TheSeaweedSite.png'
image_credit: 'Image by M.D. Guiry from The Seaweed Site' # MUST have an image credit
categories:
  - brown algae
sampling_programs:
    - keen_quad
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - bhi_farms
    - lsw_quad
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80 300915
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145856
---


**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Round and puffy potato like sac with thin, easily tearable walls.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Leathesia marina* (LEMA), also known as sea cauliflower. *Colpomenia* is a thin sac, while *Leathesia* is stiffer and more brain-like. 
See [here](https://mainenaturalhistory.org/leathesia-marina-vs-colpomenia-peregrina/) for the definitive guide.

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=145856)**
